title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Sixteen-year-old Linda Marshall experiences intense self-loathing and frustration while looking in a mirror, feeling unattractive and socially aimless. She harbors a deep, burning hatred for her stepmother, Elena, whom she views as a "beast" and a bad influence on her family. Linda observes that Elena’s presence has changed her father, Kenneth Marshall, and feels that Elena’s existence makes her life dull. Linda recalls her arrival on the island, where she met Rosamond Dunnery, a woman she respects because Rosamond treats her like a normal person. Linda imagines an ideal life where she and her father could enjoy the island's caves and beaches alongside Miss Dunnery, free from Elena's presence.

Meanwhile, Kenneth Marshall confronts Elena in her room as she prepares for an evening out. Kenneth questions whether Elena's insistence on visiting the island was motivated by her previous acquaintance with a man named Redfin. Elena claims she had no idea Redfin and his wife were coming, attributing her interest in the location to recommendations from the Larrins. Kenneth accuses Elena of wanting to "stir things up" and ruin the relationship of the young, in-love couple. Elena denies direct involvement, suggesting that while others may be attracted to her, she is not responsible for their feelings. When Kenneth asks if she admits Redfin is infatuated with her, Elena dismisses him as a fool and tells Kenneth that he should know she only loves him.
